items.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?

children.content: It's been labelled "dysfunctional" and "diabolical". What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?

items.title: Perth almost created a City of South Park in 2014 — here's why it might again

children.content: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

image.altText: Skyscrapers in the background with the Swan River in the foreground.

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

items.title: Toxic mouse bait forces school to shut for 'deep clean' in plague-hit town

children.content: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in the WA town of Morawa are sent home and the school is closed after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks is spread on the campus.

articles.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

items.title: Last private midwife north of Perth to close as costs of business rise

children.content: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

items.title: Guzman Y Gomez soars on US exit, ASX climbs higher

children.content: Australian markets have risen this morning, with Guzman Y Gomez surging on news it has pulled out of the US market. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of a yellow circle with black outlines of two faces with black writing that says "Guzman y Gomez Mexican Kitchen".

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

items.title: There are 40,000 trusts linked to Australian farms. Here's why

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

items.title: Widespread infectious disease outbreak labelled public health 'failure'

children.content: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: As coral reefs are wiped out from bleaching, this one brings hope

children.content: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

items.title: DNA study finds many dingoes may have some domestic dog genetics

children.content: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.

items.title: Global mental health disorder cases double in three decades

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

image.altText: illustration of a woman's face cut into slides on an orange background

items.title: WA gas users warn Labor not to repeat flaws in state reservation scheme

children.content: Western Australian gas users are urging the Albanese government to build stronger transparency and enforcement into its proposed national gas reserve scheme.

image.altText: Aerial shot of massive oil and gas rig in the Bass Strait off Victoria

items.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel and Memorial Days triumph at book awards

children.content: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

image.altText: Sarah Krasnostein, 48, and Chloe Hooper, 52, sit on either side of a table. Helen Garner, 83, stands between them.

items.title: Family secrets and scones highlight Australia's leading cause of death

children.content: Gwen Knox has lost her grandmother, aunt, brother and sister to dementia. The West Australian woman has now written a play that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

items.title: Friday news quiz: A pumped prince, a grinning sculpture and a stowaway frog

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings into a microphone while wearing a rainbow-coloured dress.

items.title: Early lung damage in kids with CF linked with immune cell changes

children.content: Australian researchers have mapped how lung damage may begin early in life for children with cystic fibrosis, providing new insights that may help reshape future care.

image.altText: A boy does a lung test in a room.

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor defends budget

children.content: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.

image.altText: A man in an Akubara wraps his arms around a woman with a microphone

items.title: Soft plastics industry at breaking point, as waste instead sent overseas

children.content: The soft plastic recycling industry wants more waste to stay here, saying too much material is being sent overseas.

image.altText: A white haired man with glasses, a blue collared shirt and bright yellow vest staring at piles of plastic while holding a piece.

items.title: Truck torn in half after collision with train at level crossing, six injured

children.content: Train services in Perth return to normal after at least six people, including two toddlers, are injured when a train collides with a truck at a level crossing in the city's east.

items.title: What you might not know about Pendlebury's lauded basketball background

children.content: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.

image.altText: A sepia photo of two boys wearing medals

items.title: Former Nationals candidate refused bail over alleged hit and run

children.content: The 26-year-old allegedly struck the victim with his Toyota Hilux as he was leaving for work, causing 'catastrophic' head injuries.

items.title: Man's NDIS payments allegedly spent by homesharer on holidays, massages

children.content: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer faces court charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly accessing the bank account of an intellectually disabled man who was living with him and spending over $54,000.

image.altText: A cllose-up shot of a man with short grey hair and a moustache, wearing spectacles and a dark polo shirt, outside court

items.title: Fears mount for victims of domestic violence ahead of WA court closures

children.content: The doors of 26 regional and remote courthouses in WA will shut for three months, leaving lawyers confused and concerned about consequences for domestic violence legal matters.

image.altText: The front of a court house

items.title: WA's idled nickel plants to come back to life to process gold

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.
